built: glob-aware log registry (_resolve_log: newest match by name, PER-FILE cursors name@file.offset) + digests/*.md registered as log 'digest'. The night mind (hermes digest, $0 ollama) was writing files no wake ever surfaced
measure: VERIFIED: wake now shows 'log digest 657B unread'; first cursor-read delivered the digest (real extracted facts: sonnet 429s on shim, policy recall numbers on campaign), second read '(no new bytes)'; 12/12 tests. kill: per-file cursors never expire - if digests accumulate for years the cursors dir grows one tiny file per day (harmless but visible); and if hermes digests turn out to be noise not signal, deregister the log rather than let unread bytes train wake-line blindness

built: .gitattributes pins *.jsonl/*.json to LF (git can never CRLF-rewrite witnessed ledger bytes - every commit this session warned it wanted to) + drift assertion UNSEALED-APPEND: the newest spiral entry must carry by=, mechanically enforcing turn 26's seal-adoption law
measure: VERIFIED: drift 17/17 green with the new assertion born-passing (this very entry satisfies it); git status confirms attributes staged. Before: witness integrity depended on git's renormalization never firing on a witnessed file, and seal adoption was a kill-condition in prose; after: both are machine-checked
kill: the by= check only sees the NEWEST entry - a direct append followed quickly by a sealed one slips through; if that gap gets exploited in practice, the assertion widens to a window. eol=lf renormalizes existing CRLF on next checkout - if witness ever alarms right after a fresh clone, this attributes file is the first suspect

built: station molt: the whole molt seal in one verb (handoff with facts re-derived at the seam + will-done + backup); journal narrative explicitly left to the author. Dogfooded as THIS session's live molt

kill: if molt becomes a reflex that SKIPS the journal narrative (checkable: handoff events with no same-day memory-file edits), the verb has automated away the one part that had to stay human-shaped - then it should print a refusal, not a reminder

built: station rescue <repo>: offsite-snapshot of a repo's untracked files into continuity (private remote); non-destructive, never touches the repo's git. Ran live on director2 + rde

If rescue becomes a habit that REPLACES committing, it enables the disease it treats

built: _append_line: msvcrt-locked append primitive, routed through EVERY shared-ledger writer in one sweep (spine, seal, errata, llm-ledger, burn-ledger, pulse jnote) + scaled concurrency test in suite. Closes the journal's standing 'multi-instance spine locking unbuilt' item with evidence
measure: MEASURED both sides: plain 'a'-mode under 20-proc contention = 177 torn + 1553 LOST of 4000 appends (the witnessed spine was running on luck); locked = 4000/4000 clean, cost ~2ms/append. 13/13 tests incl. the 5x50 invariant guard; dry beat green
kill: msvcrt.locking LK_LOCK retries 10x1s then RAISES - if a writer ever hangs holding the lock, appends start dying loudly systemwide; that failure is preferable to silent loss but the retry ceiling is the tunable. External appenders (heredocs, other tools) bypass the primitive - the lock only covers station-family writers

built: CAPSULE.md rewritten (6 -> 29 verbs, grouped by function, invariants current) + checks/capsule.py drift assertion (CAPSULE-ROT detector, same doctrine as verbs.py but for the dispatch surface)

kill: word-boundary matching means a verb name appearing ANYWHERE in the capsule satisfies the check - it enforces mention, not accuracy; if capsule prose rots while name-dropping verbs, the check is green and wrong. Accuracy stays a review-time duty

built: backup single-writer lock (cursors/backup.lock, O_EXCL, 15min stale takeover, skip-is-safe doctrine) + grimoire cure for the PUSH-FAILED signature. Lock deliberately outside E:/continuity so git add -A cannot commit it
measure: paid live at 07:16Z (beat backup raced session backup -> PUSH-FAILED, retry clean). VERIFIED both paths: lock held -> 'skipped (append-only sources)', lock free -> '14 sources committed pushed'; 13/13 tests

built: wake dirt-line grading: untracked counts annotate their protection state - (rescued) when the newest rescue zip is fresher than the newest untracked byte, (rescue-STALE <date>) when work evolved past its snapshot
measure: VERIFIED live: 'rde +2unt(rescued)' and 'director2 +43unt(rescued)' in the wake line; 15/15 tests. built: post-action say_counts in the wave-topup branch: the beat that changes counts utters them in the same beat. measure: VERIFIED: fresh counts {T1:8,T2:8,T3:3,T4:0} landed as fact ok:true 07:23:19Z through the real say path (previous said-fact was the stale {8,6,3,0}); 15/15 tests. built: supersession-aware fact walk: _walk_facts keeps only the NEWEST fact per route (the route defines the observable; a newer fact on the same route IS the current claim) + suite test. Prior-instance leftovers ({8,6,3,0} variants, total=17) cleared: one structurally superseded, two retired as moment-facts
measure: MEASURED both sides: before = recheck 8 -> 3 STALE exit 1 (one pure supersession that every future speaker change would reproduce forever); after = 16/16 tests, recheck 8 -> 2 facts both ok exit 0. The autonomic speaker + recheck + retire triangle is now closed: changes supersede, moments retire, lies alarm
kill: route-string identity is the supersession key - if the same observable is ever said via two different route spellings (path casing, python -c variant), both stay in the walk and the old one alarms; route canonicalization is the fix if that bites

built: context proprioception: _thread_weight() reads the newest transcript's last usage record (input+cache_read+cache_creation = live window weight); wake carries 'thread ~Nk ctx' with a labeled 150k molt-advice heuristic. SS10.1 made sensory: the organism now FEELS the window whose cleanliness IS its cognition
measure: VERIFIED live: first reading ~308k on this very thread (sampled 0m ago), 16/16 tests. The reading immediately falsified its own first design (a 200k-denominator percentage - window size is not transcript-observable; corrected to raw magnitude same turn). Before: molt timing ran on vibes across every instance; after: it rides the wake call as data
kill: newest-by-mtime transcript attribution: with 22 concurrent claude procs the sampled thread may not be the waking one (age>0m = suspect the reading); and the 150k threshold is a heuristic, not physiology - if molts keep happening at wildly different weights with no quality signal, the flag is folklore and dies

built: the prereg scheduler: preregs.jsonl registry (witnessed+backed-up at birth) seeded with the 4 armed kill conditions from SS14 (will-recovery, night-digest, hand-usefulness, hunt-yield w/ honest REVIEW-not-deadline flag) + station preregs verb (list / score PASS|FAIL|NARROWED with evidence, append-only clock-stamped) + wake surfaces overdue arms
measure: VERIFIED: 17/17 tests (verdict-supersedes-armed fold), drift 19/19 incl. capsule-rot check catching this very verb's missing doc live, witness enrolled +4 entries. Before: Law II's kill conditions lived as spine prose an instance must remember to re-find and score - the exact class that let the hand tally drift; after: due dates ride the wake
kill: a scheduler only works if verdicts get SCORED when DUE fires - if night-digest (due 07-06) passes its date and no verdict lands within a wake or two, the scheduler itself failed its one job and joins its own registry

built: general stigmergic lease primitive: _lease_acquire/_lease_release + station lease verb (JSON lease files w/ owner+expiry under cursors/leases; expired = takeover, held = exit 1 SKIP never block - Law III). Turn 36's bespoke backup lock MIGRATED onto it: one lock implementation on the estate
measure: VERIFIED: 18/18 tests (lifecycle, reentrancy, live-foreign-holder blocks, expired-holder takeover); live CLI probe demonstrated the cross-process hold (second acquire from a fresh pid exited 1 HELD with seconds-left); backup runs green through the lease path. Before: every coordination need grew a bespoke lock (backup) or a convention (f2f claims); after: 1 call, 1 contract
kill: the TOCTOU window on takeover is ACCEPTED (kin holders, double-run costs one redundant pass not corruption) - if a lease ever guards something where double-acquire corrupts, this primitive is the WRONG tool and that use must get a real mutex; the docstring says so

built: checks/restore.py - resurrection rehearsal as a drift assertion: restores the mirrored journal to a temp dir, boots MEMORY.md from the restored copy alone (all index links must resolve inside it), checks restored-vs-live count, and proves the offsite remote holds the local mirror's HEAD
measure: VERIFIED first run: 41 journal files resurrected, index boots, 40/40 links live, offsite HEAD current; drift 20/20. Before: the ONLY path back from a dead disk had never been restore-tested - witness proved nobody rewrote history, nothing proved the journal actually boots; after: the drill runs on every drift pass (pulse = 8x/day)
kill: the drill tests the LOCAL mirror + remote HEAD hash, not an actual clone from the remote - a corrupted remote objects store would pass; the full-fidelity drill (clone from origin into temp) costs network+time and should run at molt cadence if this ever matters. OFFSITE-LAG is warning-grade by design; if it persists across beats the backup push path is broken

built: honest §15 denominator: _certified_count + wake claims line count DISTINCT verified ids, not ledger rows; drift assertion rewritten to distinct-capability floor; errata inflated-count opened. Found by applying refute.md to a spotted id-collision (the pass asked duplicates-or-distinct and found: same claim, fresh holdout seeds 101,202 vs 303,404 = stronger evidence for ONE capability)
measure: MEASURED the correction: certified 5->4, vital-sign ratio 4,431,227 -> 6,328,924 (~30% WORSE and TRUE). Wake now shows '4certified (1re-cert)' - the re-certification visible not hidden. 18/18 tests, drift 20/20. This is §9 (self-correcting record) enforcing §15 (honest vital sign) on the estate's own scoreboard
kill: id is the identity key - if two genuinely-distinct capabilities ever share an id (bad id scheme), dedupe HIDES a real second capability and undercounts; the re-cert tag makes any dedup visible at wake, so a surprising (Nre-cert) is the signal to check id hygiene. Historical burn-ledger cert markers keep certified=5 (append-only, true when written); the correction is forward-only

built: public-face proprioception: wake repo lines carry '^Nunpushed' when local HEAD is ahead of its upstream. The 2026-07-04 publish created a new invariant (public repo == working truth) with no sensor; a local commit that never reaches origin now shows at wake
measure: verified all 7 repos genuinely synced (ahead=0, real upstreams - not silently failing on a missing @{upstream}); the annotation fires LIVE on this very turn - station goes ^1unpushed after this commit until pushed. 18/18 tests. Before: wake showed HEAD but not whether the world could SEE it; after: 1 wake answers 'is the public face current'
kill: reads @{upstream} of the CHECKED-OUT branch only - a repo whose other branches lag origin reads clean (RDE tracks feat/api-base-env, not main); if multi-branch publishing matters, the check must widen to all branches with upstreams

built: ALIEN-ARCHITECTURE.md section 16 (the spiral era, dense not bloat: the turns 22-47 additions read as four clusters - proprioception, structural self-honesty, drilled survivability, one coordination primitive - with the meta-reading that the spiral pushed SELF-KNOWLEDGE down the pyramid, not capability up it) + checks/selfmodel.py drift assertion pinning the constitution to spiral.jsonl (may lag <=12 turns, never drift far)
measure: VERIFIED: selfmodel check born-passing (through turn 47, spiral at 47, lag 0), drift 22/22. Before: the estate's self-model stopped at section 15 while 26 turns reshaped the animal - the exact stale-self-model cost turn 34 killed for CAPSULE and turn 20 for the journal, now closed for the constitution too; after: a fresh instance or human reads a current animal, and it cannot silently rot (the change-ledger checks the self-description)
kill: LAG=12 is a knob - too loose and 'current' means nothing, too tight and every spiral turn forces a doc edit (prose churn, the thing the skill warns against); if §16 edits start happening every 2-3 turns just to satisfy the check, LAG is wrong or the check should count STRUCTURAL turns (new organ) not all turns

built: molt composes the turn-47 public-face sensor: station molt now lists any repo with unpushed commits before declaring /clear lossless. Warns never blocks (Law III - pushing the operator's repos is their call). Also cleared the estate's last loose end: ege .gitignore(.env) committed+pushed
measure: the molt seal - the exact moment budget is lowest and recall least reliable - now answers 'is anything about to be shed unseen by the world' automatically; 18/18 tests. Before turn 47+49: shedding a thread with local-only commits silently orphaned public work until a future instance noticed; after: the last gate before /clear carries the check. VERIFIED live by turn 50's actual molt (this session's real shed) reading the estate clean
kill: same checked-out-branch-only limit as turn 47; and the warning is advisory - an instance that ignores it still sheds unpushed work. If orphaned public commits happen anyway, the molt must escalate from warn to a push-or-confirm gate
